is_p_falciparum.evidence: This is a human genetics study of the Duffy blood group locus (FY*O allele) related to Plasmodium vivax resistance. It does not study P. falciparum drug-resistance markers.

reports_target_markers.evidence: The study surveys human DNA sequence variation at the Duffy (DARC) locus; no P. falciparum drug-resistance markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfk13, etc.) are reported.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Reports human allele/haplotype frequencies at the Duffy locus, not parasite drug-resistance codon frequencies.

sub_country_location.evidence: Populations tied to specific locations: Yaounde (Cameroon), Bamako area (The Gambia), Saradidi (Kenya), Central African Republic, though these are human host samples.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No sample collection dates given; irrelevant since not a parasite drug-resistance study.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human samples from sub-Saharan Africa (Cameroon, Gambia, CAR, Kenya), but these are human host DNA, not parasite isolates.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical DNA sequencing study of Duffy locus variation.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Not applicable to the target database; independent human population genetics study.

supplement.evidence: No relevant P. falciparum drug-resistance data would be found in any supplement.

assessment.summary: This is a human population-genetics study of the Duffy blood group (DARC/FY*O) locus examining the signature of natural selection related to Plasmodium vivax malaria resistance. It studies human host DNA, not P. falciparum parasites, and reports no P. falciparum drug-resistance markers or frequencies. Entirely out of scope.
exclusion_reasons: Not a P. falciparum study — examines the human Duffy blood group locus (P. vivax resistance)
Samples are human host DNA, not P. falciparum field isolates
